ab: A valɨvalɨke nɨme ka iké namɨ sdanɨ Oponoho, sullte nɨme nga ka, suwanga aula Takau, iké lɨdreke sékacu mia.
ch: 小時候我們家住在萬山，上學以後，就搬到了高雄，一直在都市裡生活。
en: When we were young, our family lived in Oponoho. After I started school, we moved to Kaohsiung and have been living in the city ever since.

ab: Muili sulte daikakusé nga, la kbuburu poké sesa na kɨcarisia sulate, rigu ku Taiuan kɨcarisia makacuku nga na margi bunka.
ch: 上了大學以後，我修了一堂關於原住民文化的課程，瞭解到臺灣原住民族有各式各樣豐富的文化。
en: After I went to college, I took a course on aboriginal culture and learned that the aboriginal people in Taiwan have various and rich cultures.

ab: Sukéni cacɨvélé tébarbarvia ka, kɨkɨ si kaka sovlé su siviri nga namɨ cɨkɨlɨ kbuburu marudrɨngɨ, tarigu ku cuku ta si bunka ta mia.
ch: 因此，今年暑假時，我和哥哥回到部落訪問老人家，想要瞭解自己族群的文化。
en: So this summer, my brother and I went back to our tribe to visit the elderly, hoping to understand the culture of our own ethnic group.

ab: Kɨné marudrangɨ ka mvarlu cevél dra, makasi cnglɨ madɨ armé na thɨbarɨ la na ico.
ch: 老人家已經八十歲了，但是看起來還像中年人。
en: The old man is already eighty years old, but he still looks like a middle-aged man.

ab: Kibuburu kɨdria aci ku ariguasu na bka, urugu Pupunganɨ, mathuré psobaké dra, laigu Kunadavan bka, Auba si Sodé na bka.
ch: 我問他會哪些語言，他的萬山語說得很流利，也會說多納語、青葉語和霧台話。
en: I asked him which languages he knew, and he was fluent in Oponoho, as well as Kongadavane, Japanese and Vudavudadhane.

ab: Kiburuburu kɨ dria laigu kɨ, avalɨvalɨke dra ka kiburu buru Pupunganɨ bka, asiradra ka Kunadavan ico, tikadra kamlu ka madro so Auba ico, sukédra rigu makabaka nga.
ch: 我問他才知道，原來他小時候就學萬山話，太太是多納人，而他工作上認識許多青葉人，所以他會這麼多語言。
en: I asked him to find out that he learned Oponoho language when he was a child, his wife is an Kongadavane, and he knows many Japanese at work, so he can speak so many languages.

ab: Tika nɨme mabka dria, ki marudrangɨ la porkéké ululupu na sakiké sirigu.
ch: 我們訪談的時候，老人家談論了許多關於狩獵的傳統知識。
en: During our interview, the old man talked about a lot of traditional knowledge about hunting.

ab: Ipta pɨdrɨngɨ namɨ piciringi rigu, makasi iké nɨma dénva saké luing mia, duru pɨtapɨdrɨngé ligiri rigu.
ch: 雖然我們沒有辦法完全聽懂，但是我們有用手機錄影，可以再仔細聆聽。
en: Although we could’t fully understand it, we could record it on our cell phones and listen to it more.

ab: A valɨvalɨke li ka irigu kɨ cuku li si bunka li, langirigu kɨkɨ kcarisian kɨ mia ikeela maurumalɨ matɨ.
ch: 小時候我不瞭解自己族群的文化，對於自己是原住民有點不好意思。
en: When I was young, I didn’t understand the culture of my ethnic group, and I was a little embarrassed that I was an aborigine.

ab: Rigu mdɨnga ngédro ka, la rigu madɨ Drekai cuku ico mia, sakwa angula, uduru madɨ pkiluvu.
ch: 瞭解愈多以後，愈覺得自己身為魯凱族人，是很獨特的，也很值得驕傲。
en: The more I know, the more I feel that being a Rukai is unique and something to be proud of.
